critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — LOW-VOLUME PERFORMANCE: 19.4% MAPE at bottom quintile. Full autonomy for these stores is unjustified for perishables.

C2 — NO OVERRIDE: Architectural gap for perishable exception handling.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — Acknowledge. 19.4% MAPE is materially above the 8.3% overall. For perishables, this level of error creates operational problems. Tiered deployment (full autonomy for top 3 quintiles, restricted or human-reviewed for bottom quintile) addresses the concern.

C2 — Concede. Some minimum override mechanism is needed for perishable goods, especially during high-demand periods.
adjudication_raw: Both points validated. Tiered deployment and minimum exception process required. Mixed verdict — deployment is supportable with modifications.
